steps.title: Identify the shared preference
steps.content: The passage states that both herons prefer areas with standing water for more than 60 days per year. This shared preference is the key to identifying what neither species would be drawn to.

steps.title: Apply the preference to the blank
steps.content:
Choice A directly names the opposite of their shared preference: areas with far fewer than 60 days of standing water.

This is the condition they would logically avoid. The other three choices introduce unrelated factors: other species' breeding seasons, features unique to one heron type, or water depth that only one species values.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
